response: Do not believe the myths that vocational training is less valuable than a traditional degree. Those who choose vocational paths are often seen as less ambitious, less intelligent, and less committed to their future. They are told they are settling for a lesser life, that they are not working hard enough to earn a four-year degree. In reality, they are simply making a different choice—one that may not fit the narrow expectations of society. Do not assume that those who take vocational routes lack the drive or potential of their peers. They may be more practical, more skilled, and more ready to contribute to the workforce. Let us not judge by the type of education someone has, but by the impact they make. Do not look down on those who have chosen a different path; they may be the ones who truly understand what success means. Do not look down on them. Do not look down on them.
